_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — take the L7 'Footman Sergeant' composited keeper and produce the L8 'Footman' variant (Tier 3 mid).
history: v1 (2026-05-06): initial author — Footman beat = 5 visible adds (half-visor on helm covering upper half of face with horizontal eye slit, FIRST GOLD ACCENT as gold rivet heads at the seam between the layered 2-disc pauldrons, 3-LAME PAULDRONS adding a third lower lame extending down to cover the bicep — silhouette mightiness rule visibly broadens shoulder line, longer 3-lame tassets extending to mid-thigh with slight outward flare at lower edge, engraved cuirass medial ridge with fine-line scrollwork from collar to belt). Within-tier chained input strategy. Livery: skirt band narrows from ~3 inches at L6/L7 to ~2 inches at L8 as tassets extend longer.

v2 (2026-05-07): revised per AD FeedbackV0 — FIRST GOLD MOVED to a HELM TRIM BAND (clear gold band wrapping the helm at the brim line) instead of gold pauldron-seam rivets (the L8 gold-rivet pass soft-failed at thumbnail per Archer drift table; a band lands cleanly). TASSETS revised from 3 narrow lames to 2 WIDER LAMES per tasset per AD detail-economy. Add FULL CONTINUOUS SABATONS at L8 (toe + foot top + ankle continuous steel) per AD 'boot armor split into smaller upgrades' — final boot-armor stage. REMOVED the engraved cuirass medial scrollwork add per AD detail-economy ('rely on shapes and silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree') — the cuirass already gained volume at L7 (medial ridge + horizontal seam); adding scrollwork on top was decoration accumulation. CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION updated: cuirass is now medial ridge + ONE simple horizontal seam (not 3 lames), tassets at L7 are 2 wider lames, hip pouch is the only belt accessory (no knife sheath), L5 full bicep-to-wrist scale mail preserved, L6 ankle plates only (no full sabatons until this L8 step).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub stale L7 "~16 rim band studs" PRESERVE references — L7 add was rewritten to drop the rim-band stud accumulation per AD detail-economy; surgical pass also dropped remaining rim-stud language.

v6 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27.

adds.title: HALF-VISOR ON THE HELM
adds.summary: add a steel half-visor extending DOWNWARD from the brim of the existing sallet helm to cover the UPPER HALF OF THE FACE (forehead, eyes, eyebrows, upper bridge of nose).

adds.title: GOLD TRIM BAND WRAPPING THE HELM AT THE BRIM LINE
adds.summary: add a continuous BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL band wrapping the helm at the brim line all the way around (front, sides, and back).

adds.title: 3-LAME PAULDRONS
adds.summary: ADD A THIRD LOWER LAME extending downward from the existing 2-disc pauldron base disc.

adds.title: LONGER 2-LAME TASSETS EXTENDING TO MID-THIGH WITH OUTWARD FLARE
adds.summary: extend each existing tasset DOWNWARD to cover mid-thigh (each tasset now ~9 inches tall, up from ~6 inches at L6/L7).

adds.title: FULL CONTINUOUS STEEL SABATONS ON THE BOOTS
adds.summary: add cool silver-gray steel covering the FRONT of each boot from toe through the central top of the foot up through the ankle as a CONTINUOUS metal plate.

image_urls: <<UPLOAD: UnitProgression/Infantry/out/v1/L7/composite/infantry_L7_v1_composited.png>> (content reference — apply the upgrades onto this character)
prompt: CRITICAL PRESERVATION VERIFICATION (READ FIRST) — The input image is the canonical content reference. It already shows the 'Footman Sergeant' kit accumulated on top of the existing base. You MUST preserve every prior feature shown in the input image: (a) the OPEN-FACE STEEL SALLET HELM with forward-projecting brim worn over the mail coif (the new step ADDS a half-visor BELOW the brim — the helm dome and brim themselves remain unchanged); (b) the FULL STEEL CUIRASS — at reduced; the cuirass remains preserved at with NO engraved scrollwork added (the engraved scrollwork add was REMOVED 2026-05-07 — late-tier richness comes from volume + silhouette shape + material change, not stacked surface ornament); (c) the WIDE WARM-BROWN LEATHER WAIST BELT with square dark-iron buckle visible between the tassets at the front; (d) the STEEL TASSETS at both hips — exactly 2 WIDER LAMES per tasset (NOT narrow multi-lames); the new step EXTENDS them longer to mid-thigh and grows them to 2 wider lames per tasset (still 2 wider lames, just longer); (e) the LAYERED 2-DISC STEEL PAULDRONS on both shoulders (the new step ADDS gold rivet heads at the seam between the two layered discs AND ADDS a third lower lame extending down the upper arm to cover the bicep); (f) the STEEL VAMBRACES on both forearms extending continuously into STEEL GAUNTLETS at the wrist (knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates, no skin visible on the hands); (g) the SOLID FLANGED STEEL MACE HEAD with 6 vertical flanges (NOT wooden, NOT spiked — the existing swap from wood+spikes to flanged steel must remain); (h) the WOODEN MACE HAFT with leather grip cord on upper third and iron reinforcement strip running its length; (i) the LARGER STEEL-FACED ROUND SHIELD with embossed sun-burst boss (raised central dome with 8 short triangular rays radiating outward), the four iron cross strap-bands radiating from the boss, the continuous iron rim band (clean wider band, no studs); (j) the existing IRON TOE-CAP STRIP on the front of each boot AND the existing STEEL ANKLE PLATE on each ankle (at boot armor was iron toe-cap + steel ankle plate ONLY — the new step adds full continuous toe+foot+ankle sabatons per Edit Goal item 5, completing the boot-armor schedule); (k) the LIVERY SKIRT BAND visible through the front gap between the two tassets (the new step NARROWS this band as the tassets extend longer, but the red+mustard split is still visible as a narrow ~2-inch band); (l) the BROWN LEATHER HIP POUCH on the anatomical-RIGHT hip — the ONLY belt accessory, NO knife/dagger sheath; visible at the upper edge of the right tasset, riding above the extended tasset top edge; (m) the existing FULL BICEP-TO-WRIST SCALE-MAIL SLEEVES on both arms (chunky overlapping rounded scales) — sleeves cover the entire arm from shoulder seam to wrist with vambraces overlaying wrist-to-mid-forearm, no exposed bicep gap; (n) the MAIL COIF back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm. DO NOT revert any of these features. EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible additive upgrades to the infantryman shown in the input image, marking the mid Tier 3 step from Footman Sergeant to Footman. The unit takes its first gold accent, gains a half-visor that begins concealing the face, and broadens the shoulder + tasset envelope (silhouette mightiness rule active: shoulder line widens with the new third lame; tassets flare longer and gain articulated lames). (1) HALF-VISOR ON THE HELM — add a steel half-visor extending DOWNWARD from the brim of the existing sallet helm to cover the UPPER HALF OF THE FACE (forehead, eyes, eyebrows, upper bridge of nose). The visor is cool silver-gray polished steel matching the helm body, with a single horizontal eye slit running across the visor at eye level (the slit is approximately 1 inch tall, the warm steel-blue eyes are STILL VISIBLE through this slit as two bright eye points within the slit's shadow). The half-visor's lower edge sits at approximately the bridge of the nose, leaving the LOWER HALF of the face visible: the prominent slightly-hooked nose tip, the dark mustache, the full short-trimmed dark-brown beard, and the chin remain fully visible below the visor's bottom edge. The visor has a soft three-dimensional contour following the curve of the upper face, with two small dark-iron rivets at the visor's hinge points where it meets the brim above each eye. The brow ridge of the visor is slightly raised. (2) GOLD TRIM BAND WRAPPING THE HELM AT THE BRIM LINE (FIRST GOLD ACCENT ON THE UNIT — moved from gold pauldron-seam rivets at this level/gold helm trim at to a SINGLE clean gold helm trim band at + Archer soft-fail learning: gold rivets at pauldron seam don't read at thumbnail; a continuous helm band lands cleanly) — add a continuous BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL band wrapping the helm at the brim line all the way around (front, sides, and back). The gold band is approximately 1 inch tall, sits at the seam between the helm dome (above) and the brim (below) at the front, and continues around the sides and back. CRITICAL gold rendering: the gold MUST render as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL, saturated warm-yellow hue contrasting strongly against the cool silver-gray steel of the helm — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint, NOT a warm-tinted highlight on steel. Render the gold with: (i) a saturated buttery-yellow base tone (warm yellow-orange like polished 18-karat gold, distinctly more yellow than the surrounding cool gray steel); (ii) a soft top-down highlight that reads almost white-yellow; (iii) a deeper warm shadow on the underside that reads honey-orange. The band must be IMMEDIATELY READABLE as gold at thumbnail scale. This is the FIRST gold accent on the unit; the helm trim band is the ONLY gold added at this level (NO gold pauldron-seam rivets, NO gold elsewhere at this level — other gold accents arrive at later levels). The dark-iron brim rivets remain visible AT THE BRIM (below the gold band). (3) 3-LAME PAULDRONS — ADD A THIRD LOWER LAME extending downward from the existing 2-disc pauldron base disc. The new third lame is a curved steel plate covering the upper arm from the lower edge of the base disc down to roughly mid-bicep, ~3 inches tall, cool silver-gray steel matching the pauldron, with two visible dark-iron rivets at its upper seam where it joins the base disc. Each pauldron now reads as 3 stacked plates: small upper disc (smallest), base disc (medium), bicep lame (longer downward). The shoulder envelope reads visibly BROADER and LONGER than at this level — the silhouette mightiness rule applies: the shoulder line must clearly extend further outward and downward than at this level. The new third lame covers the previous scale-mail short sleeves on the upper bicep (the scale-mail short sleeves are still visible briefly at the lower edge of the lame as a narrow ~1-inch band of steel scales just above the upper edge of the steel vambraces). (4) LONGER 2-LAME TASSETS EXTENDING TO MID-THIGH WITH OUTWARD FLARE — extend each existing tasset DOWNWARD to cover mid-thigh (each tasset now ~9 inches tall, up from ~6 inches at the prior level). Each tasset remains exactly 2 WIDER LAMES stacked vertically (an upper wider lame and a lower wider lame), each lame ~4.5 inches tall × ~6 inches wide, with a thin shadow seam between them suggesting articulation. CRITICAL: each tasset is exactly 2 wider lames, NOT 3 narrow lames, NOT 4 — wider plates, fewer of them. The lower edge of each tasset has a SLIGHT OUTWARD FLARE so the bottom corners are wider than the top corners — the tasset silhouette flares outward at the bottom. The cool silver-gray steel matches the cuirass. The two tassets are still separated at the front by a narrow vertical gap (now ~2 inches at this level, down from ~3 inches at this level); the LIVERY skirt band remains visible through this narrowed front gap as a ~2-inch tall red+mustard strip. The dark blue trousers are still visible at the calf below the lower edge of the longer tassets. (5) FULL CONTINUOUS STEEL SABATONS ON THE BOOTS (final boot-armor stage — completes the existing iron toe-cap → ankle plates → full sabatons schedule) — add cool silver-gray steel covering the FRONT of each boot from toe through the central top of the foot up through the ankle as a CONTINUOUS metal plate. The iron toe-cap and the existing ankle plate are now joined by a steel plate covering the top of the foot between them — toe + foot top + ankle now read as one continuous steel sabaton silhouette. The brown leather body of the boot is still visible at the calf above the sabaton AND at the heel/sole; ONLY the front-and-top of the foot is now continuously metalled. Two visible dark-iron rivets at the seam where the sabaton meets the leather. The sabaton has a subtle articulation crease where the foot bends at the instep so the plate reads as flexible steel, not a single rigid block. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion: his chiseled chibi jawline (still visible below the new half-visor); his stern flat closed-mouth expression (mouth visible below the visor); his thick dark-brown eyebrows (NOW HIDDEN BY THE HALF-VISOR — eyebrows are concealed at this level); his large warm steel-blue eyes (NOW VISIBLE ONLY through the horizontal eye slit as two bright eye points in the visor's shadow); his prominent slightly-hooked nose with the rosy painted tip (the upper bridge of the nose is concealed by the visor's lower edge; the nose tip remains visible); his warm peachy-tan skin tone with rosy blush (visible on the lower face / cheeks below the visor); his FULL SHORT-TRIMMED DARK-BROWN BEARD covering chin and lower cheeks (visible below the visor) — trimmed short, NOT bushy, NOT wild; his dark mustache (visible below the visor); his dark-brown short hair fringe (NOW HIDDEN BY THE HELM AND VISOR — no fringe visible at this level); the mail coif's back-of-neck and shoulder drape behind/below the helm; the bevor / upper-chest steel; the LIVERY skirt band visible through the narrowed front gap between the two tassets; the wide leather belt with iron buckle; the brown leather hip pouch on anatomical-RIGHT hip; the brown leather knife sheath with bone-handle knife on anatomical-LEFT hip; the steel vambraces extending into steel gauntlets with knuckle-plates and articulated finger-plates; the leather grip cord on the upper haft; the iron reinforcement strip on the wooden haft; the wooden mace haft (the mace HEAD is the existing flanged steel head — wooden head + spikes are GONE); the larger steel-faced round shield with embossed sun-burst boss, four iron cross strap-bands, continuous iron rim band (clean wider band, no studs), central steel boss; the steel sabatons over warm-brown leather boots; the dark blue / muted steel-blue trousers visible at the calf. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, body angled approximately fifteen to twenty-five degrees to the viewer's left, weight planted evenly on both feet with slight forward lean. THE MACE ARM (anatomical-RIGHT, viewer-LEFT) extended forward and slightly DOWNWARD, gauntleted hand grips the upper haft, flanged steel mace head dangling at hip height angled down. THE SHIELD ARM (anatomical-LEFT, viewer-RIGHT) bent at elbow, round shield up at upper-chest / shoulder height. Calm guard stance. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, warm subsurface skin (lower face only), saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal. Clean thin black silhouette outline in deep warm brown-black with even weight. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Match the framing of the input image as closely as possible (the first input has slight tier-break growth from L1_Base — vfill ~70% — that growth is acceptable; the new features should not push framing further). DO NOT zoom in further, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT crop, DO NOT shift. Clean white background — no scenery, no ground plane, minimal contact shadow, 1:1 square aspect ratio. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT close the visor fully — the existing visor is a HALF-VISOR covering only the upper half of the face; the lower face (nose tip, mustache, beard, chin) MUST remain fully visible (closed visor arrives at this level); DO NOT cover the eyes opaque — eyes must still be visible as bright steel-blue points through the horizontal eye slit; DO NOT make the eye slit a vertical slit or a cross slit — single horizontal slit; DO NOT extend the visor below the bridge of the nose; DO NOT remove the helm dome / brim — the helm body is preserved; DO NOT add horns, crest, plumes, or any vertical helm extension (horns arrive at this level, central crest at this level); DO NOT extend the gold to other parts of the unit — gold at is ONLY the helm trim band wrapping the helm at the brim line; DO NOT add gold rivet heads at the pauldron seam (the existing first-gold beat MOVED from gold pauldron rivets to a clear gold helm trim band + Archer soft-fail learning — gold rivets at thumbnail don't read; the helm band lands cleanly); DO NOT add gold buckle, gold knuckle-plates, gold mace, gold shield rim, or any other gold at this level (gold expands at later levels); DO NOT change the gold color to brass or bronze or yellow paint — gold MUST render as buttery POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL; DO NOT make the third pauldron lame a fourth disc — exactly 3 stacked plates per pauldron at this level (4-lame arrives at this level); DO NOT cover the entire upper arm with the lame — the lame ends at mid-bicep with the existing full bicep-to-wrist scale-mail visible below it under the vambrace; DO NOT extend the tassets beyond mid-thigh; DO NOT make the tassets one continuous skirt — they remain TWO SEPARATE plates with a vertical gap at the front; DO NOT make each tasset more than 2 wider lames — exactly 2 wider rectangular lames stacked vertically per tasset at this level (NOT 3 narrow lames 'rely on shapes and silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'); DO NOT shrink the livery skirt band to nothing — it must remain visible as a ~2-inch red+mustard strip through the narrowed front gap; DO NOT add engraved scrollwork / filigree / etched leaf-and-curl motifs / chased lines on the cuirass medial ridge or anywhere on the cuirass — the engraved cuirass scrollwork add was REMOVED 2026-05-07 ('rely on shapes/silhouettes, not sculpted details/filigree'); the cuirass at is exactly the existing cuirass (raised medial ridge + ONE simple horizontal seam) with NO new surface decoration; DO NOT add any heraldic emblem, cross, or sculpted motif on the cuirass at this level; DO NOT add a closed visor, full-face visor, or face cover other than the half-visor; DO NOT change the round shield to any non-round shape; DO NOT change the boss color to gold — the sun-burst boss remains cool silver-gray steel at this level (gold ring around the boss arrives at this level); DO NOT add a cross emblem to the shield — that arrives at this level; DO NOT change the mace head shape — the existing flanged steel head with 6 vertical flanges is preserved; DO NOT make the mace head gold or partially gold — gold mace arrives at this level; DO NOT replace the wooden haft with metal — the haft remains wood; DO NOT change any facial feature visible below the visor; DO NOT grow the beard bushy or extend it past the upper neck; DO NOT remove the beard; DO NOT change the trouser color or boot color (boots remain warm-brown leather where the sabatons do not cover); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT swap the hand holding the mace; DO NOT swap the hand holding the shield; DO NOT change the camera angle, framing, or composition; DO NOT crop the figure; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, ground plane, or cast shadows; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, or non-white; DO NOT drift the art style toward anime, photorealism, fully painterly, flat shading, cell shading, or pixel art; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
